title: coap_server.c: handler return values ignored
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_server.c
Lines: 126-145, 165-184, 232-251, 271-329, 349-368

Several CoAP handlers return response codes directly but the Zephyr CoAP service expects handlers to return 0 on success or negative errno on failure. The handlers mix return patterns:

```c
static int status_get(...)
{
    if (s_handlers.status == NULL) {
        return COAP_RESPONSE_CODE_NOT_FOUND;  // Returns positive CoAP code
    }
    ...
    return build_response(...);  // Returns coap_resource_send result
}
```

COAP_RESPONSE_CODE_NOT_FOUND is 0x84 (132), which is positive and may be misinterpreted by the service framework. The handlers should send a response via build_response and return 0, or return negative errno on failure.

Category: API MISUSE - incorrect return value semantics
close_reason: Fixed: all coap_server handlers (status_get, config_*, neighbors_get, key_get, msg_inbox_*) now call lichen_coap_respond/send_ack and return 0/negative errno only (no positive COAP codes). Updated coap_dtn.c, coap_msg.c, coap_config.c, coap_keys.c, coap_location.c resource defines to lichen_coap_server for consistency. Logging standardized to LOG_ERR. Full coap_*.c + tests fixed.
